headline: How The Essent Group (ESNT) Story Is Shifting With New Targets And Buybacks
summary: Analysts recently adjusted their fair value estimate for Essent Group from US$67.81 to about US$69.14, a move of roughly US$1.33 per share. The shift reflects a blend of slightly higher and slightly lower price targets, as analysts fine tune how the stock’s current price compares with their views on earnings execution, revenue quality, and capital efficiency. As you read on, you will see how these moving targets and fresh data points can help you track the evolving story around Essent Group’s...

headline: Bank Of America ATM Fee Settlement Puts Focus On Valuation And Risks
summary: Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) has agreed to pay US$2.25 million to settle a class-action lawsuit over ATM fees at 7-Eleven locations. The lawsuit alleged that customers were charged excessive interbank fees for single transactions at 7-Eleven ATMs. Bank of America denies any wrongdoing but chose to settle the case, drawing fresh attention to fee practices at large US banks. For investors watching NYSE:BAC, this settlement fits into the wider discussion of how large retail banks earn fee income...

headline: How Investors Are Reacting To Salesforce (CRM) After Bank of America Flags AI Monetization Challenges
summary: In mid-May 2026, Bank of America reinstated coverage of Salesforce with an Underperform rating, arguing that the company faces a structural reset as AI reshapes its core CRM and agentic offerings. The bank’s critique that Salesforce may be shifting from a high-growth story to a mature cash generator highlights a tension between its aggressive AI investments and questions over how effectively those tools will be monetized. We’ll now examine how Bank of America’s skepticism about Salesforce’s...

headline: BofA Study Finds Fewer Gen Z Rely on Family for Financial Assistance, Even With 42% Living Paycheck to Paycheck
summary: According to the Bank of America 2026 Better Money Habits® financial education study, 81% of Gen Z (ages 18-29) say it's important to be perceived by others as financially responsible, and fewer are relying on family for financial assistance. Today, 34% of Gen Z report receiving some form of financial assistance from their parents or other family members, down from 46% in 2024. When examined more closely, while 51% of younger Gen Z (ages 18-22) receive financial assistance, this drops to 29% for

headline: Investors' Allocation to Bonds Falls to Four-year Low in May
summary: Investors cut allocations to bonds to their lowest in almost four years, Bank of America's May global fund manager survey says. Their exposure to bonds was cut to a net 44% underweight, the biggest underweight since June 2022, the survey says.

content.title: Investors' Allocation to Bonds Falls to Four-year Low in May

headline: Global Semiconductors Seen as Most Crowded Trade: BofA Fund Manager Survey
summary: Fund managers say investing in global semiconductors is the most overcrowded trade across global markets, Bank of America's Global Fund Manager Survey finds.  Close to three quarters of money managers surveyed said that semiconductor stocks were the most crowded trade in May, up from around 25% in April.  Last month, fund managers said the most crowded trade was betting that oil prices would rise.

content.title: Global Semiconductors Seen as Most Crowded Trade: BofA Fund Manager Survey
content.summary: Fund managers say investing in global semiconductors is the most overcrowded trade across global markets, Bank of America's Global Fund Manager Survey finds.  Close to three quarters of money managers surveyed said that semiconductor stocks were the most crowded trade in May, up from around 25% in April.  Last month, fund managers said the most crowded trade was betting that oil prices would rise.
